name: Conflict Minerals Regulations and Due Diligence Frameworks
description: Compilation of conflict minerals regulations covering 3TG (tin, tantalum, tungsten, gold) and responsible mineral sourcing frameworks

regulations.name: EU Conflict Minerals Regulation

scope.description: EU importers of tin, tantalum, tungsten, and gold (3TG) above de minimis volume thresholds
scope.coveredEntities: Union importers of minerals or metals containing or consisting of 3TG

volumeThresholds.description: Annex I of the Regulation sets volume thresholds; importers below thresholds are exempt
volumeThresholds.note: Thresholds vary by mineral/metal form (e.g., tin ores: 5,000 kg/year, gold: 100 kg/year)

scope.excludes: Downstream manufacturers using 3TG in finished products are not directly covered but encouraged to conduct due diligence

importerObligations.name: Management system
importerObligations.description: Adopt and clearly communicate supply chain policy for minerals and metals from conflict-affected and high-risk areas (CAHRAs)

importerObligations.name: Risk management
importerObligations.description: Identify and assess risks of adverse impacts in the mineral supply chain based on available information

importerObligations.name: Third-party audit
importerObligations.description: Carry out independent third-party audits of supply chain due diligence at points in the supply chain where the risk of adverse impacts is greatest

importerObligations.name: Disclosure
importerObligations.description: Report publicly on supply chain due diligence policies, practices, and risk assessment outcomes

memberStateObligations: Designate competent authorities for ex-post compliance checks
Carry out ex-post checks to ensure importers comply with obligations
Publish lists of recognised supply chain due diligence schemes

cahras.definition: Areas in a state of armed conflict, fragile post-conflict areas, and areas with weak or non-existent governance and security, including failed states, with widespread and systematic violations of international law including human rights abuses
cahras.indicativeList: European Commission publishes a non-exhaustive, indicative, regularly updated list of CAHRAs
sourcesForIdentification: Reports from civil society and research organisations

regulations.name: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform Act — Section 1502
regulations.legislativeReference: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act, Section 1502 (15 U.S.C. § 78m(p))

scope.description: SEC-reporting companies (issuers) for which conflict minerals are necessary to the functionality or production of a product manufactured or contracted to be manufactured
scope.coveredMinerals: Tin, tantalum, tungsten, gold (3TG) and their derivatives
scope.coveredCountries: Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and adjoining countries: Angola, Burundi, Central African Republic, Republic of the Congo, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia
scope.applicability: Only SEC-reporting companies (public companies filing under the Exchange Act)

formSD.name: Form SD (Specialized Disclosure)
formSD.filingDeadline: Filed annually with the SEC by May 31 for the prior calendar year
formSD.description: Issuers must file Form SD describing their RCOI and, if necessary, due diligence measures

exhibits.name: Conflict Minerals Report
exhibits.requirement: Required if RCOI does not resolve that conflict minerals did not originate from covered countries or did not finance armed groups
contents: Description of due diligence measures performed
Description of products manufactured or contracted to be manufactured that are not DRC conflict free
Facilities used to process the conflict minerals
Country of origin of the conflict minerals
Efforts to determine the mine or location of origin

rcoiProcess.name: Reasonable Country of Origin Inquiry (RCOI)
rcoiProcess.description: Initial inquiry to determine whether conflict minerals originated in covered countries or from recycled/scrap sources
steps: Determine whether company manufactures or contracts to manufacture products containing 3TG
Determine whether 3TG are necessary to the functionality or production of the product
Conduct RCOI to determine country of origin
If RCOI indicates minerals may originate from covered countries, exercise due diligence per OECD framework
outcomes: If RCOI determines minerals did NOT originate from covered countries → disclose in Form SD, no Conflict Minerals Report required
If RCOI cannot determine origin or indicates covered country origin → perform due diligence and file Conflict Minerals Report

oecdGuidance.name: OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas
oecdGuidance.issuingBody: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD)
oecdGuidance.description: International standard for supply chain due diligence of minerals from conflict-affected and high-risk areas, referenced by both EU and US regulations

fiveStepFramework.name: Establish strong company management systems
requirements: Adopt and commit to a supply chain policy for minerals from CAHRAs
Structure internal management to support supply chain due diligence
Establish a system of controls and transparency over the mineral supply chain
Establish a company-level or industry-wide grievance mechanism

fiveStepFramework.name: Identify and assess risk in the supply chain
requirements: Map the factual circumstances of the supply chain
Identify risks by assessing factual circumstances against standards in the Guidance's model supply chain policy
Assess risks of adverse impacts in light of standards set out in Annex II

fiveStepFramework.name: Design and implement a strategy to respond to identified risks
requirements: Report risk assessment findings to senior management
Devise and adopt a risk management plan
Implement the risk management plan, monitor and track performance
Undertake additional fact and risk assessments for risks requiring mitigation, or risk mitigation where a risk is identified

fiveStepFramework.name: Carry out independent third-party audit of supply chain due diligence
requirements: Have supply chain due diligence practices audited by independent third parties
Audits may be conducted at industry level through joint audit arrangements
Audit scope should cover all elements of the due diligence process

fiveStepFramework.name: Report on supply chain due diligence
requirements: Report publicly on supply chain due diligence policies and practices
Reports to include steps taken to implement the five-step framework
Report on identified risks and steps taken to manage those risks

responsibleMineralsInitiative.name: Responsible Minerals Initiative
responsibleMineralsInitiative.description: Industry initiative (formerly Conflict-Free Smelter Program) providing tools and resources for responsible mineral sourcing

programmes.name: Responsible Minerals Assurance Process (RMAP)
programmes.description: Third-party audit protocol to assess smelter/refiner management systems and sourcing practices against RMAP standards, aligned with OECD Guidance
programmes.scope: Smelters and refiners of 3TG, cobalt, and mica

assessmentOutcomes.description: Smelter/refiner has demonstrated conformance with applicable RMAP standard
Smelter/refiner is actively undergoing assessment or re-assessment
Smelter/refiner has been assessed and found not in conformance

conformantSmelterList.description: Publicly available list of smelters and refiners that have been assessed as conformant with RMAP standards
conformantSmelterList.updateFrequency: Updated regularly as assessments are completed

programmes.name: Conflict Minerals Reporting Template (CMRT)
programmes.description: Standardised reporting template for sharing information about the origin of 3TG in supply chains
programmes.usage: Widely adopted by companies to collect smelter/refiner information from their supply chains

programmes.name: Extended Minerals Reporting Template (EMRT)
programmes.description: Reporting template extending beyond 3TG to cover cobalt and mica
